lines.x: It's me.
I've reached the target destination. I have visual confirmation of the smoke signal's coordinates.
...It's been a while since our last fight with the other mercenaries. We've taken some losses.
But they hid plenty of supplies in the underground bunker. We won't come back empty-handed.
Mm, yeah. The scout can go on ahead.
I'll catch up soon.
...Right, there was an unexpected casualty.
W is dead.
You're talking like it was hell on earth out there, but you look perfectly fine to me.
Or are you talking it up to get a pity bonus?
Well, it's not like you had to go out of your way to leave camp to pick us up. Who asked you to be such a worrywart?
I don't give a shit about anyone. Quit projecting.
If W hadn't stayed behind to create an opening for us, none of us would've made it out.
...When did it happen?
Two hours ago, when our communications were cut off. The squad got ambushed.
W blew apart the ruins and gave us a chance to escape. He fought until the bitter end.
Poor guy.
Yeah. If only he made it back to camp alive. His kill count was well past mine. He would've come out as top merc, easy.
Forget it, no point talking about that stuff now. At least he's earned his rest.
With the enemy routed, we should probably bring him back?
Impossible. We can't risk exposing ourselves like that. Don't want to get caught out.
Why would you even want to? Were you two that close? Nobody told me.
He had a bunch of loot on him. That's all I care about.
It wasn't that much. Some of the other guys brought in bigger hauls.
When the war ends, we can go back to the good old days.
...If the war ends.
Hmph...
We can sit around and commiserate later. We'd better get out of here before sunset if we don't want to be the center of attention.
Head back to camp and muster the troops.
Hmm—?
Is that an order, 'Second' Lieutenant Hoederer?
...We've lost contact with the other squads. As of right now, I'm your commanding officer.
We're the same rank.
*Sigh*....
...Ines, let's hurry back to the rendezvous point. Then we can contact the client again to renegotiate our deal.
That's a suggestion, not an order. All right?
Hmph...
How much you think we can milk W's death for?
A lot.
He was a helluva merc. Everybody knew.
At least... his death won't be in vain.
In the end, did he leave anything—
—Shh!
Someone's coming. Three o'clock. Not one of ours.
Just one. Get out.
A Sarkaz...? One of the natives?
No, wait... You have W's gun and knife in your hands...
Who the hell are you?
Not talking? Then, die.
Wait, hold up.
She followed us here.
...You let her follow you?
We haven't exactly been dragging our feet, but she's kept up with us purely through her own skills, even after grabbing W's gear.
She's an 'experienced' Sarkaz, and I may need a local guide when it's time to bounce.
—Are you insane? Isn't that all the more reason to kill her right here?
If you want us all dead, you might as well just do it yourself.
I wouldn't dare.
What if she's an assassin? What if she leads us straight into a trap?
Did you forget how many people in Kazdel want your head?
Oh? How many?
...There's one right in front of you.
You've got a huge bounty on your head. But for the time being, I'd rather it stay on your shoulders. Don't get too full of yourself.
I appreciate your diligence and frugality, but I'm not joking around either.
She took the risk to pick up W's knife and gun, and had the stones to walk right up to us.
What are your Arts telling you? Is she hostile?
...No sane person would just take in a totally unknown Sarkaz.
Yeah, that's the difference between you and me.
On my way back, I gave her a whole bunch of openings, and she... threw little stones at me.
An interesting display of good will, wouldn't you agree?
...Huh?
According to the old ways, we might want to give her a chance.
This war has thinned out our ranks. We could recruit some more Sarkaz, just as mysterious as her, but I'd rather hand-pick someone myself.
But, she's an outsider. Our ways don't apply to—
—You know what, forget it.
I'm leaving in ten minutes. I don't care how many come with me.
But if you're gonna give me any more corpses to deal with, I don't care whose, you better make it quick.
Heh. Quite the impatient one, aren't you.
...All right, you. Listen carefully.
You're holding our fallen comrade's relics there.
Put them down, and you can walk out of here alive. You'll have to find somewhere else to die, but at least you'll buy yourself some extra time.
This is your last chance.
Last... chance?
...I don't think I ever had a choice. The second I turn around, you draw your blade and cut me down.
This whole time... your other hand has been on that hilt.
...Very good.
You may have seen us before, or maybe you worked for someone else. None of that concerns me.
But, you understand what it means to take up the weapons of the fallen, yes?
Of course.
In that case, prepare to evacuate first. We'll talk details later.
Now, go rejoin the squad—
There is no end to war. And there is no end to our fight.
It is as if war is the tool that we have always relied on for survival.
...Mm, I am convinced of that.
Some still harbor reservations in their heart, while others have grown accustomed to that fact. The latter live, the former die.
And some people... were born to play this role.
What a pleasant surprise.
When I saw her in those crumbling ruins, I knew for sure...
She would reign supreme among the Sarkaz warriors.
Several months later...
4:28 P.M. \ Cloudy
Military buffer zone between Kazdel's east/west battlegrounds
A small ambush.
Looks like you were right. A lot of people do seem to be after my head. What a pain.
Not too many of them. They must be trying to cut off the head of the snake in a hit-and-run.
We only have half a squad left. What a perfect time to try and take me out.
...Too bad for them it's a little too perfect. Just as planned.
All right, I'll catch up with you later. The Messenger for Kazdel should be back in a minute.
...What are you looking for?
That corpse... that's the assassin?
...Yeah.
I knew he'd have it on him.
A note...?
Actually, wait, did you know this guy?
Of course.
We've worked together before. Went through hell together. Just a few months ago, we were at the Scar Market, toasting to a job well done.
He even said he wanted his daughter to marry me.
That's dumb. You're mercenaries. Sooner or later...
Hold on, you didn't really—
Of course not. I turned him down.
...For other reasons, of course.
So, what does it say?
Useful stuff. Some intel from the Market. Gossip. Things worth looking into.
...And what about this?
They're all names... No, all pseudonyms or code names. And there's... candy drawn on the back of this? What's all this supposed to mean?
The more pieces of candy, the higher the payout. It's a system only a piece of shit like him could come up with. Whoever gets the most candies is the most dangerous mercenary.
Creeps like that really leave a bad taste in your mouth.
...But then again, this adds up to a fat stack. Who's footing the bill?
Every time we complete a contract, someone will usually put up an additional sum to get rid of us. Like whoever hired us, for example.

The more powerful the mercenary, the bigger the bounty. The bigger the bounty, the greater the risk. The greater the risk, the more they need to kill off the merc. And the mercs who live through all this... they become the strongest.
This war has driven us to the brink of death, but it's also left room for us to survive.
I guess that all works.
It's the rules of the game.
... 'Hoederer, Mercenary, 10 candies. Tough squad, 15 candies. We're acquainted, 20 candies.'
Can't fault his accounting.
'W, the old one, taken care of. The new one is a problem. 10 candies, maybe more, situational.'
Sounds like you earned your candies too.
Might have something to do with those arms dealers I buried alive in the woods last month.
Hah... So, am I supposed to be happy about this?
Arms dealers, huh... Why am I learning about this just now?
I was just in the neighborhood.
When you guys raid the Laterano caravans, do you bother reporting each and every one?
No.
Right? It's the same idea.
Since this isn't technically a part of the job, it's not like us mercenaries have to share every little detail.
Hmm... But lemme get this straight. I'm stronger than that old 'W,' huh?
Strong or weak doesn't tell me shit about a soldier's actual capabilities. For me, it's all about whether or not they can follow orders.
Fine, fine. I'll be more thorough next time. Does that work for you?
By the way, I've been wondering, what kind of person was that old 'W?'
That guy... was a strange one.
He was with us for a long time. Hardest worker you ever met, a real good egg.
He fought hard to become our team leader, racked up kills and commendations, just so we'd celebrate his birthday.
...A Sarkaz? With a birthday?
Of course it wasn't his real birthday... He wasn't that far gone.
He once killed a Laterano and took his gun. I guess they had some history between them, so he decided that day was his birthday.
Mhm... A merc who killed Lateranos for sport, huh? Sounds like we could've been friends.
There's a lot to unpack in there.
But, 'for sport?'
Hey, gotta live in the moment.
Good answer.
'W' was one of our biggest characters.
He always had that devil-may-care laugh, but whenever he spoke, you could tell that there was something more lurking underneath.
But he was quicker to trust than pretty much anyone else.
Why's that?
Because he had an 'obsession.' Obsessed people, good or bad, are always falling into traps.
...All right. Just sounds like a dumbass to me.
I figured you'd get it..
Because you're the same type of person. Good at putting on airs, good at living in the moment.
I am?
...Hah.
What makes you so confident you can guess what I'm thinking? Or, do you have some weird Arts like Ines?
You...
You could've decided to kill us both that day. I thought you understood that?
*Sigh*... You're not going to let that go, are you? Reminds me of Ines...
Let's head back and see how our Messenger's doing.
It's about to rain.
That's the first time I saw her smile. The same smile as W's.
Giving her a thinly-veiled excuse was at least better than having her continue this numbingly dull busywork.
What I needed was a mercenary who could cleave a path forward, not just another puppet. Puppets are a dime a dozen..
That was the day W became W.
...But Ines was right.
Ridiculous.
Ines, you should head back to the tent. No use staring blankly at a campfire.
Hmph. Can't imagine we got good news from Kazdel.
Nail on the head.
I could feel the Messenger's anxiety after he came back. He tried not to show it, but he was scared out of his mind.
...Not to mention your expression. Your poker face couldn't fool a toddler.
So, what's the bad news?
So, I'm still alive, but... our agent died in Kazdel's industrial area.
His corpse got thrown into a furnace. His head got melted off, but those gaudy red pants are somehow still fine.
Who did it?
Nobody knows when it happened. First person to find the body was one of the boiler workers.
But, whoever did it was quick. You have a fight on metal and it's gonna show for a while—
They used a knife. And they were damned good with it.
Whatever. It's not like we'll ever catch them.
He wanted to hang out in Kazdel and didn't like to take orders. And this is what happens. Kinda funny, huh?
But now who's gonna pay us? I'm not laughing.
And the next part is even less funny.
When our Messenger was leaving Kazdel after confirming the report, he ran into some friends...
Some... spies.
I guess it's more 'was intercepted by' than 'ran into.'
...So, we've been caught again?
Wouldn't be the first time. We could take the initiative here...
By the look of things, we don't have a choice. The Messenger came back with an order.
...An order? For us? From who?
Since when do we take orders from anyone?
I've gone through the options. Just let me finish.
They want us off the battlefield and up to a remote mountain pass. Looks like it might take us through a canyon.
...To protect a transport team.
Escorting transports is a pretty run-of-the-mill job. There has to be more to it, right?
...I don't know.
...Excuse me?
Really sorry. The situation's spiraled out of control—
But this could be a rare opportunity for us.
Now that we're responsible for the entire team, the situation has changed. Everyone else is looking for an opening, and we can't just sit around waiting to die.
You and W are in charge of this job.
...I have some issues here.
First of all, I won't go with W.
She's made some waves, but I don't think she's ready yet.
She's totally proven herself.
I see things you can't. I trust my Arts.
Hoederer's team shows up with a new demolitions expert, and suddenly the battlefield is one big fiery explosion.
The other mercs are all playing it cool. The 'battlefield' changes every day, and you never know when it might swallow up your base camp.
...I know.
She doesn't hesitate; not when she's lying, not when she's killing. She is too 'qualified' a mercenary. I can't tell how deep she is.
I'm not looking for a knife in my back, and I don't think she's cut out for this 'escort' job. No matter how great you think she is, now's not the time to send out your greenhorn.
Or did you have some ulterior motives?
I'm working with the tools I've got.
Look at where we are. We can't leave people on the sidelines anymore.
Mercs don't have a dog in the fight, really. But that also means we don't belong anywhere. When the dust settles, we'll fade away, along with the war.
The difference is, war will return one day. But we have only one chance to live.
I'm not going to be fatalistic about this... and neither should any of you. That's the deal.
We have to play the hand we've been given. Stand out from the crowd, and stake out our own position.
...That group's here from Rim Billiton. I'm willing to bet they get raided not long after crossing into Kazdel territory.
A lot of our people won't take the job. We'll have to change the teams up a bit, but it doesn't matter in the end.
It's a dangerous contract, but worth the risk. We, or at least you—
Hold up.
Not that I question your decision... It's just that...
...Ah, forget it. Anyway, you don't need to be so uptight. I'll follow your orders, as always.
But first, you need to calm down, then take a look at your own feet.
Oh... Are you talking about my shadow?
I can't read your eyes. What am I looking for?
Your shadow. It's trembling.
...It's windy. And the campfire is flickering.
You know what I'm talking about. All you Sarkaz are like this.
Don't always try to plan too far ahead. There are too many things you can't control. We're just mercenaries, after all.
...I'll keep that in mind.
So, if you want me to bring W with me, what about you?
What are you planning?
That's on a need to know basis. You're safer this way.
If you can't trust the other teams, you should at least trust your own.
I'm not hiding anything from you, it's just...
Out with it then.
Spill it!
Who was it our Messenger met?
...'Babel.'
A sleeper cell Babel left in Kazdel.
Tsk... Those are the same goons as last time— How'd they find us here?!
Hurry up and retreat! Don't waste time!
They've got us surrounded. And outnumbered.
Then we gotta find a way to break through!
Their scouts took out all our sentries. We're in the dark here...
Bullshit! Are you gonna stand around and wait for their Casters to fry you?
Hey, you... You're with Hoederer... What are you doing here?
Where's Hoederer? And his men? He's supposed to be—
Wh-what are you doing to my comms?! What gives—!
...Would you shut up already?
Remember how I was on patrol here? That made it real easy to plant bombs all over your camp.
...You! Hoederer! You sold us out...!
Don't make it sound so bad.
We're headed out on a long, long trip. And wouldn't you know it? Some guests showed up just as we're leaving. So someone's gotta stay behind to hold the fort.
Now if we leave some snacks out for our guests like a good host, maybe that'll get them to stop chasing us?
Gotta say, I appreciate all you're doing for us~
Damn you... You knew they were coming the whole time... And you're just going to leave us here to die—?!
Hmmm...
Well, at least try to put up a bit of a fight. The more you struggle, the bigger our head start.
But seriously, thanks a kiloton.
I ordered you to help the teams that didn't have time to escape, but...
...Why didn't anyone else make it out?
They volunteered to stay behind.
Don't worry. I left them a little gift... So they were more than happy to help us hold off the bad guys.
...I doubt anyone here would be willing to sacrifice themselves.
No need to lie about it. Nobody's going to blame you for sacrificing others to save your own squad.
But lying to your commander is another story.
You can't fool my eyes, W. You're on thin ice here.
Don't forget how green you are.
...I'll be sure to reflect upon my actions, Captain Ines.
Because we'll be working together... for a very long time.
So, what's the plan now?
No harm, no foul. What's done is done, and it was probably going to turn out like this anyway.
W might have made the right call.
...Do you seriously believe that?
The Sarkaz over there have nothing to do with us.
Who they were, what they did. How they lived, how they died. None of our business.
Brutal.
Let's talk work first. Ines, you and W split up and lead your teams away. Messengers stay radio silent, then regroup at the rendezvous point.
...I'll catch up later.
...One more thing.
I don't care what kind of mischief you get up to, how one of you exploits the other or whatever, but I won't stand for you actually, directly fighting.
But indirectly is fine?
Pretty lax rules.
...*Sigh*.
I really hope it doesn't come to that. But it's not like I could stop it either.
For now, at least, if you plan to stay alive, make good use of every last Sarkaz you have.
Hoederer has always been like this.
He thinks very little about what actually matters, and wastes so much brainpower on the pointless stuff.
...Nobody says W isn't a great fighter. But there's a lot she's missing.
If she really is just an incredible Sarkaz warrior...
....Hmph.
It's not even dark out. And you still gotta hide over here by the fire?
Maybe you just like fire?
I definitely don't like people bothering me.
Your team is three hours late.
That ambush... did you and Hoederer plan that?
Go ask him.
He doesn't know anything about it.
...Then it must've been a coincidence. All mercs are a pain in the ass to deal with. You cross their path, and they're gonna give you shit.
Oh, that's all? Here I was worried Hoederer sold out my position.
You think that's what he should've done?
Why not?
How do you know we're not lying to you?
You could've killed me a long time ago. Doing it now wouldn't help much, would it?
Hmm...
What were you doing before you found us?
Huh? After all we've been through, you're giving me the job interview now?
Hoederer never asks about his people's pasts. But I'm not Hoederer.
Yeah you're a whole different species from us.
Are you looking for trouble here?
As far as I can tell, 'Miss Ines' isn't so good at close combat.
A lesson for you: If you want to live a little longer, don't put all your eggs in one basket.
Every Sarkaz mercenary carries a sword.
Well color me surprised. All this time I thought that was your staff. But...
—Oh, but you, a 'Sarkaz mercenary?'
—Tch...
I'm still pretty sure I've got you beat at close range, but... Hmm, I admit I got a little careless.
Well, thanks. A scratch on the arm is a pretty cheap price for that lesson.
You and Hoederer are both pretty good at keeping your cards close to your chest.
I'd rather talk about you. High-profile mercenaries like you are pretty much all dead by now.
You've made such big waves in such a short time, but I never heard anything about you before you joined us. That's pretty suspicious.
And you came looking for Hoederer. Why?
You said it yourself.
What?
A lot of people in Kazdel are after his head.
You dropped that bomb pretty smoothly. Why'd you change your mind?
Hmm... I figured I'd get what I want easier this way.
And what is it you want?
...None of your business.
You're shook. How funny. Why not make something up to dodge the question?
...Your Arts again?
Those damned eyes. My reasons have nothing to do with you.
Same with my eyes.
What was that?
...From the east. Something's coming.
There's more than one squad, but only one of them's the real deal. Hmph, they sure are cautious...
Ehh, sounds like a pretty big gang. Just how many people did they hire?
...Hoederer was right. This is going to be our biggest battle yet.
We're heading into uncharted territory now.
You scared?
Hah.
Hoederer should be here soon with his squad. Tell the Messenger to get back on comms.
I don't remember anyone putting you in charge. Don't give me orders.
Oooh, whoopsie...
You just keep guarding this campfire over here. I'll head down to the camp and check on the situation.
That's... Babel's...
Sorry, had to take a little detour along the way. Ended up wasting a bit of time.
But it's time for us to really get to work now.
Positions, everyone.
Hm... And how you doing over there?
A-okay.
...Judging from the burnt grass and neatly-cleaved mud here, you two have been busy.
C'mon, we were just sparring a little, as a warm up. Definitely not fighting or anything.
Right?
Don't worry. Next time, I'll bury you where they'll never find your body.
...*Sigh*.
Just to reiterate, our job is simple. Don't let anyone near our mark.
Eliminate anything in the canyon that looks at you sideways. Open fire on anyone who approaches.
Any questions?
So, where's our destination?
Can't disclose that. This escort task is to be carried out in stages, and our mapped route has already been passed out. After we reach our destination in three days, someone will connect with us.
...Hoederer.
What are they transporting?
I also can't— Wait, Ines.
It's a breach of contract to use your Arts to probe the transport team. Don't underestimate them! Stop right now!
You said they were a regular old transport team—
I... no, this is definitely a convoy that needs protection.
But this huge thing that they're transporting... I'm only seeing a shadow...
...A landship? No, it's...
...a skeleton?
To the north of Kazdel lies a birch forest.
There, the progression of life unfolds. From Spring it begins, and in Winter it ceases.
And only death thrives in the long winters. All living things make their exodus in unison, leaving only the Originium crystals speckled along the ground to reflect the somber moonlight.
...Pale, withered tree trunks hang lifelessly, their slender shadows intertwining and stacking upon empty expanses of snow.
That is the scenery I saw.
...Ines!
...Ines! Tch!
...Screw you!
If you're not dead yet, then wake up already.
Huh...?
(My head... what is this pain...?)
What's going on... what happened?
Nothing happened, but just in case that fall gave you amnesia, I'll give you a quick summary.
We were ambushed an hour ago. They were quick. Our caster had no time to put up a barrier, and our team got split up. We're the only ones here.
And the comms?
Jammed. I don't know how they're doing it, but we're definitely up against pros. At least more professional than any of our prey before.
But the good news is they didn't go straight for the convoy. Looks like they got a tip or something, but not the details.
...Go ahead. Find a way to meet up with the others.
Oh? I figured you'd doubt me, at least a little.
Guess I'll leave you as bait.
Who do you think would survive if we fought?
Obviously—
Tsk... You really should do something about that nasty personality of yours. You can tell I'm injured that easy?
Oh... so it hurts that bad, huh?
I figured you had some control over your emotions, like you could fake them. Guess I was being more careful than I needed to be.
Do you always scan your teammates with Arts as soon as you wake up? Now that's a mark of trust, boss.
Force of habit.
So that's why, huh? The first thing you do after you open your eyes is surveil your surroundings. What a coward~
Hey! That hurts...!
You said bait, right? Not a bad idea.
No point in dragging along a merc who can't even stand up straight. You stay here for a sec and I'll call in the enemy.
Hope you guys get along nicely. Can't have you dying on me too quickly.
See you later, W.
Oh, one last thing. I never trusted you.
Hey, you little—!
...I found a mercenary. Just one.
Confirmation. It's W.
...That was fast.
Or rather, you've been following us the whole time, huh...?
Letting a few of them go was well worth it if it means we can get our hands on an HVT like you.
Hmph. I was gonna follow you back to the rest of your team. Never expected them to just abandon one of their own.
Tell me everything you know about them and you might get a quick death.
Well there's the trouble, huh? ...I'd love for us to be honest with each other, but the truth is that I don't know anything.
You're badly wounded. And we don't have time to waste. You might die if we torture you on the spot, and I'm sure nobody wants that.
In that case, you should chase down that woman. She knows more than I do.
...Don't you resent her for using you as bait?
You'll have a better time if you work with us.
...Bait, huh?
Say... Have you ever baited Originium slugs with industrial residue before?
...What are you talking about?
It's kind of a Kazdel folk thing. We get Originium slug infestations, you know?
Wild Originium slugs are pretty dumb, and they like Originium—
—But setting that aside, do you know what happens to the poor slugs that are lured over?
Don't mince words with—
They get blown apart, just like your buddies over there.
What?! When did you...?!
Hey, you even said I was bait. Didn't you think that meant a trap?
Probably should've killed me sooner, or ran off, I guess. Here, I'll give you another chance. Let's take it from the top.
*cough* *cough*——
—So fast?! Have you been chasing us this whole time?
You're insane!!
Ugh... Who's... behind...
Hush. No sudden movements.
It's kind of a beginner sword, but I'm not so good with a blade. If my hand slips, you die.
When— When did you get—
I'm pretty close with the shadows. Did you really think you'd be able to catch me without a scout?
Speak up. Who's your master?
I'll... never—
Graaaaahhh!!
Easy there. How's a guy supposed to talk if you turn his organs to mush?
Kazdel will... never... forgive you...
(Kazdel...)
Mm, thank you.
You really suck at torture.
...Damn. You're still alive.
You could've let him kill me first. Too bad you're so impatient.
Hmph. Of course I'd rather kill them first. These insects have taken too much from us.
When the enemy shows you an opening, any merc worth her salt would kill him, no?
...Hah.
You really do have a bit of Sarkaz in you, huh?
Rubbed off on me.
That wasn't exactly quiet. I bet more of them are on the way.
Isn't that what we want? Keep them away from the objective.
He said he let a couple get away... That means the rest are dead.
He wasn't good enough to take down that many of our people. And he was definitely scared. There's... something else out here.
Whew.
...Were you really faking your injury?
No no, you can't just fake an open wound away.
This is all mind over matter.
But let's talk about you. Did you really know that I had a present ready for them as soon as you woke up?
If I didn't wake up, I would've been the bait.
I definitely would've waited for you to die before springing the trap.
Hmph...
If one day, you meet someone who actually could read your mind...
You won't be a match for her.
I guess I'll cross that bridge when I come to it.
Can you feel around with Arts?
...As if I wasn't going to do that already.
No...
That's the look that usually means we're boned.
You got that right.
But there's no running now. We press on.
And you suck at fighting, so it's all up to me?
Head west. We might be able to turn this around with Hoederer and his team.
And if we can't find them?
Then we die.
We wiped out half the escort team, but we couldn't stop the convoy before point A5.
They threw out a lot of decoys. Some of the most well-guarded ones were fakes.
Either Babel's pulling some next-level strats, or... the real one doesn't even need protection.
Yeah, we need to end this fast.
Thought we could leave mop-up operations to those scum... Didn't think they'd blow that too.
Their skills are all over the place. Hoederer's team was a lot tougher than I thought.
If Babel didn't score them first, would've been nice to get them for ourselves.
Do we keep chasing?
We don't know what's up ahead.
Understood, General.
We lost them?
Split up. You go east, I'll take the west.
Phew... phew...
These guys aren't easy marks.
Duh. Hide.
Pfah...
Knock it off. You've been at it since the beginning. You're gonna go blind. Don't your eyes hurt?
Of course they hurt, dumbass. But we have to find a way out...
Aieeeeee! I surrender!
Ooh, what a nice, clean slash. Did you see that?
They're dressed up as mercs, but these aren't mercs.
Cut the chatter!
They don't even need to ambush us. They could take us head on in the ruins and we wouldn't be able to beat them.
What are you thinking?
Huh...
Are you still spying on me? At a time like this?
No. I just haven't gone blind yet.
You've got so many... are those grenades? What are you doing?
Are you gonna blow yourself up with them?
A kill's a kill, no?
Don't throw your life away for nothing.
For what then?
What's the point of living? What should I throw my life away for?
...Sarkaz really are crazy.
That's why I never trusted you. You're not crazy.
Hey, W! Wait!
Gah, come back!
Yep. Found one. The one and only W.
The way you toss those Originium grenades around, and such big ones too. That's the kind of crazy that gets my blood pumping.
How do you do it? All my friends incinerated in a flash, and don't have a scratch on you?
What can I say? Practice makes perfect.
The General's gonna like you.
Just lay it all out for me. What's Babel got you doing? You can do a lot better somewhere else.
Why should you trust me?
Why should I trust you?
Asking the right questions in the right order.
Mercenaries are a tool of war. If we're gonna fight, we gotta have the right tools at hand.
Don't you think we're more expendable than that?
That's your call.
We got another one hiding 17 meters out, 4 o'clock. The one with the scouting arts. You can think it over too.
Put the sword down. You can't beat me. Welcome to try though.
Hmph...
What do you want?
Yeah... this fits. We got W and Ines.
You got maybe 20% of your people left. The scattered remnants can't do much.
What's that you're standing on...?
Oh? This?
It used to be a person. I guess it's hard to tell at this point.
You get it now? We're not your regular mercenaries. We're not hitmen either.
What if we do this? There's the two of you, plus your leader. Only one of you three has to die.
The other two bring back a head. I don't care whose. We pin all the losses on the head and take in the survivors.
Not that this is my call. But we have to do something for our fallen men.
Things could've gone a different way if we weren't meeting on the battlefield.
That's awful nice of you.
Just my luck. I suck at fighting. I probably can't beat this ugly bitch.
Now we're so honest all of a sudden.
Yeah, so the only other choice is for us to team up and kill Hoederer.
Yep. Hoederer.
So——
Hm...
Tell me... why do mercenaries insist on getting killed?
I'm not gonna stay on the side that's getting played. I'd much rather play with someone else's life.
Plus, I'm real sick of listening to you. You're boring. And your shitty little attempt at a death game sucks.
Oh, and my poor little lamb here steadily going blind...
She's not Sarkaz. You noticed, right? You stepped right into her minefield.
You know how this will end. You've already lost.
Oh?
I dunno about that one...
Are you really going? The landship is right here.
Yes, I didn't expect them to spot it so soon. Did intel blow this one?
At least we've got the mercenaries.
We put them in danger. They have no idea what's happening.
They're mercenaries. They live for this. And sooner or later... it will be our burden too.
I can save them. I can save some of the men who would die for our mistakes.
You're right, Theresa, but...
There isn't a single Sarkaz, not a single person, who should die for nothing.
...*Sigh*... Then I'm coming with you.
Good. Thank you——
——Kal'tsit.
Wow, reinforcements, huh...? Who are they...?
There's a lot of them...
Hey, you stay with me.
Sorry... I'm just so... sleepy...
Since when do you know the word 'sorry?'
I had to watch you pass out last time... Now it's your turn...
C'mon, be a team player... I just need a little... nap...
Tch.
Fourteen, fifteen... Still more...
They're professionals. Look how they move... they know the battlefield even better than we do...
...Kazdel... hmph...
Ine... Come in... someone...
Going... to save you...
Comms are back...?
What... what's this feeling...?
Someone's close...!
...Hoederer.
Ines! Where's W? What are your coordinates?
You lived in Kazdel... before the war... didn't you?
There's something I have to ask...
Are you wounded? Catch your breath.
Find a safe place to hide. The convoy is going to call in reinforcements for us...
Just... listen to me, Hoederer.
Okay, I'm listening.
The King of Sarkaz... The one who lost Kazdel... Her Majesty...
Is she... a woman with white hair?
The Sarkaz stopped.
The burning ruins collapsed in towards the center. Those monstrous creatures looked down on us from above.
A woman suddenly emerged from the center of the circle. A very... strange woman.
She wasn't Sarkaz. She stood in the center of the battlefield, her expression unchanging.
Ines was trembling, but I couldn't tell you why.
The explosion must have damaged my hearing. I couldn't hear what they were saying.
But I knew they were afraid.
Was it because of her?
No, their attention was focused in another direction, on someone else.
In the seconds before I lost consciousness, I saw——
A... Sarkaz.
An unthreatening... slender Sarkaz.
They... for the moment...
Sarkaz... must...
...You will... what?
She is heavily injured... If we don't do something now...
...If... he... finds this place... We're out of time...
...Kal'tsit, give me a hand.
...*Sigh*. Fine.
Where am I...?
On a landship.
Landship...?
How are we still alive?
If you're gonna screw around, can you leave me out of it?
*Sigh*...
Long story short, we got rescued.
So where are we?
The main convoy. This is the landship we were protecting. Now it's hauling our wounded.
Looks like it's still under construction. I can't tell how it all fits together. Weird.
(But the shadow I saw... It was... Some questions are better left unanswered.)
What about Hoederer?
He's with the client... Err, he's... talking to the boss of this place.
Right. If you're still hanging around here, Hoederer must be fine.
Tch.
So what's the deal with 'the boss of this place?'
You look nervous. And I don't need to blind myself with Originium Arts to figure that out.
Don't tell me you think I'm hanging out here because I care about you?
Definitely not. So what's the reason?
*Sigh.*
I guess I just feel bad for him.
Who knows what kinds of monsters are in that room with him...
Try to relax. Nervousness will only drag down our efficiency.
You did the best you could. The leak was our fault.
We don't really look for fault on the battlefield. I know who I'm dealing with.
Good.
But I can't say I expected that commander... to be one of Her Majesty's own.
You're... a lot stronger than I expected. I haven't had a chance to thank you yet, Dr. Kal'tsit.
Theresa's the one you should be thanking.
What was that about me?
Your Majesty——
We're not in Kazdel anymore. Let's not stand on ceremony. We're here to sit down and talk, Hoederer.
By your command.
And, the one in the coat there must be...?
I hope you don't mind. It's very important that the Doctor be well aware of any new intelligence.
Thank you for understanding.
(It really is a landship... But it's pretty big, huh...?)
(There are parts that look new, but some bits are so old they're practically falling apart...)
(I think... this is from Rim Billiton...)
Oh! S-sorry. They're still building this place...
Dr. Kal'tsit said... not to go any deeper. It's dangerous...
Uh huh...
Okay. I'll go somewhere else.
Thanks.
What a nice kid. With those ears, she's definitely not Sarkaz.
Come to think of it, that one lady who sucks even more than Ines, that doctor, she isn't a Sarkaz either...
What's going on here? I look around in the middle of a battle and nobody's Sarkaz?
How could you be jammed again?!
Huh?
Happy place, Closure. That's only the seventh short circuit today...
THE——SEVENTH——SHORT——CIRCUIT——
Kind of embarrassing for a genius engineer, huh? What if we invested in a new set of doors? That'd save some time!
Oh... well, we have so many wounded to care for. Their provisions are rather expensive, in both manpower and resources...
And wouldn't giving up be an even bigger embarrassment...?
Waaaahhhh——
Okay! Your Majesty!
Give me three days! If I can't fix it in three days, I'll take the whole security control system apart and reinstall it!
Re-reinstall? You can do that?
I dunno who built these mysterious systems and then buried them out in Rim Billiton somewhere, then if we can't figure it out...
We'll just have to replace it with something we can! Easy peasy!
Well... I'll leave you to it then.
Roger that!
...*Sigh*.
Rhodes Island... how long will it be until you're up and running...?
Rhodes Island?
Oh...
You're... I remember, you were with Hoederer...
You've regained consciousness? Your wounds were quite serious.
(Did I scare her...?)
I'm W, the mercenary. I'm looking for Captain Hoederer and I kinda got lost...
Be careful where you wander. This place is still being built. There are a lot of exposed wires. You could get shocked.
But I have a feeling such caution is second nature to you, no?
W, was it?
You may call me Theresa.
Hoederer had words with the Doctor after our meeting. I believe he should be back in his room in Medical by now.
Don't make them worry. You ought to head back too.
Theresa... Your Majesty?
Oh? Well, we're not in Kazdel anymore. No need for titles.
Okay, Theresa... Majesty, why were you wrestling with a door just now?
And that short girl there is an engineer, right? I mean, it's just a door...
It's not just a door.
O... kay?
How many things are there totally unworthy of my attention?
So what's Rhodes Island?
Oh, you heard that.
That is the name of this vessel. Although it has not yet been officially christened... that is the name I wish for it.
Maybe it's just my whimsy. The Doctor and Kal'tsit may reject its 'true name.'
...Heh. True name? For this landship?
You saw. This vessel wasn't built in Kazdel.
Searching deep in the oldest records we have, I came across that name...
'Rhodes Island.'
I'm not actually sure what it means. Hm... Kal'tsit should know more.
But that is what I want to name it. It already had a name, after all.
(Her Majesty Theresa fought against the Regent who usurped Kazdel... She personally brought together more than half the Sarkaz clans...)
(...and she's fixing a door herself?)
What's wrong? You have an odd expression on your face.
Would it be considered improper to laugh in front of Your Majesty?
N-no... But neither Kal'tsit nor the Doctor are the laughing type... and the warriors have too much on their plates for such frivolity.
I would quite like it if you all would smile.
But that is perhaps an extravagant hope. I understand the pressure I've placed on everyone.
So I'm cool to laugh then?
Um, yes?
Nah, the magic's gone. Can't do it...
Why do I feel like that's my fault?
Hehe... well, I wouldn't dare.
What about you?
What about me?
I am Theresa, this is Rhodes Island, and you are?
That's not what I mean. 'W' is a mercenary codename. I want to know your true name. The one that represents you..
You need not cloak yourself in a disguise tainted by war. This goes beyond names. Let's just say I'm nosy.
Oh... forgive me.
You don't have a name?
It's pretty common for Sarkaz born in Kazdel.
We don't really care much for... names. People forget them all the time. What's the point in making an effort to remember a name?
I don't want to forget them. I can't forget them.
If you wait for the dust to settle in Kazdel... until you're no longer 'W,' we could speak like this once again.
Maybe then you could have a more fitting name. A proper name for a Sarkaz woman like yourself.
Like Rhodes Island. A name makes things feel so much warmer, doesn't it?
I never really thought about it...
Theresa, message.
Kazdel is on the move. The Doctor is getting ready.
Right, I'm on my way.
W?
Oh, yeah, I'm here.
If you are willing to fight for us still, you will always be welcome here. No matter what.
...*Sigh*.
I'm not going to criticize Theresa's decision, but...
Don't expect too warm a welcome. I've seen your record. You're dangerous.
Oh. Same?
You're recovering fast, but not enough to have you wandering around as you please.
Go back to bed before we have to have someone carry you there.
I realized later.
I never dared to look Theresa in the eye.
Why?
She looked so naive.
It wasn't the look of a leader at the head of one of the armies in our cruel war...
Is naive the word...?
You don't have eyes like that when you're naive... Such... sad eyes.
And those two were always at her side, it seemed.
If... if I could stand with them...
What kind of sights would I see?
(That hood... is that the 'Doctor' they were talking about?)
(Is that person... looking my way...?)
(What the... Why...?)
(Am I scared? Scared of what? That person gives off pretty normal vibes, but...? No, it's too vague. I've never seen anyone that weird before...)
(Oh... that's Babel's battlefield commander. I remember now...)
('The Doctor,' huh?)
I'm starting to get why Ines wouldn't go near Her Majesty.
Maybe it would be better to look out for myself every once in a while.
But... I'm not sure why... Ugh, when it comes to Theresa, I never really knew why...
Call it a whim. Hoederer and the others would understand.
You've decided.
Sounds like you aren't asking, Dr. Kal'tsit... Sorry, I just realized, I'm supposed to call you Lord Kal'tsit.
There's no point in talking about that now.
I just want to express my disappointment at your decision. You could have been a great help to Theresa.
Her Majesty... came to me alone, after the last mission.
Sounds like her.
She said she remembered me, where I came from, in Kazdel.
The huge industrial district, and the endless rotten, crime-ridden city of death that stretches out around it.
The influx of refugees growing the slums up skyward.
How many 'nobles' do you think there are in Kazdel? How many of them are Sarkaz who gave themselves some stupid title in an even stupider war?
As many as there are weeds in a garden.
That's a good one. They are like weeds.
But Her Majesty might remember.
I appreciate her kindness. She remembers the last bit of humanity left in the Sarkaz.
She's the only one who could.
So why are you trying to escape?
Put it a little rougher, why don't you, Doctor?
Ines, Dr. Kal'tsit has done a lot for us. You know that.
Hmph...
No offense intended.

You don't need me to tell you that.
Right.
But what about the other Sarkaz?
W has her own plans.
And it's not like we have the power to make every mercenary follow the rules.
That's how mercenaries are. It's a lot harder to keep order with these free spirits than with big standing armies.
But some lost Sarkaz warriors could stand to learn from you... that which you seek.
Regardless, you've chosen your path.
Even if it's a dead end.
Us mercenaries only know how to dive into war, to hide ourselves in the smoke from its wake of ruin.
...I hope that works out for you.
Thanks.
...There are barely any Sarkaz still following us.
A lot of them decided to stay, but there's even more who don't get to pick where home is anymore.
The camp's gone. The only equipment left is the bottom of the barrel stuff Babel left us.
We're more like campers going mountain climbing than mercenaries at this point...
...It's always been like that, all the way back to when we first met in that birch grove and decided to carve our own path.
Good thing Dr. Kal'tsit gave us a few special contracts. At least we'll be able to get by.
If we're still going to be running errands for her, what difference does it make whether we leave Babel or not?
We're just a team of mercenaries fighting to make a profit, taking a lucrative contract.
You're lying to yourself.
...Wait, where's our flag?
I kept it. And everything else. If I'm gonna lie to myself, might as well be thorough about it.
You remember right? Back then, whenever a standard-bearer fell, another would always step up right away.
From the west to the east, becoming a mercenary, then all the way back here. That whole time, this flag never fell.
Up until the 70th, or maybe 80th standard-bearer died. He impaled his own abdomen with the flagpole to keep it up.
When we were escorting the Rhodes Island landship, those enemies... those monsters from Kazdel, shredded our lines like paper.
When that happened, it was just a kid holding the flag.
The enemy had the high ground. Arts rained down around us like a mudslide.
Before Dr. Kal'tsit arrived, there was pretty much nothing we could do to fight back.
The flags are all in tatters.
Yes...
But I've still got hands. I can hold up a flagpole.
I never liked flying a merc flag.
It should've been stomped into the mud a long time ago.
How many vets from the old days are still alive?
You, and me.
...Let's not waste any more time.
Hey, Hoederer.
Did you leave W with Her Majesty on purpose?
What do you think about Her Majesty? And Kal'tsit? And especially... that 'Doctor?'
Never took you for the gossipy type.
Just making small talk.
You didn't use your Arts once the whole time you were there. That's not like you. I was worried maybe—
...I didn't have the guts. Not with the way Dr. Kal'tsit was looking at me.
She's an unknown quantity.
Right, better not dig too deep. Just knowing who that Kal'tsit is...
